violations.text: The ceiling is in poor repair, with tiles missing exposing spun insulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025

Packs of French fries in the market are lacking labels. Food sold in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC stated he will remove them from the premises today. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2024

The door to the outside, located in the market, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the hot water tank and shelves in the basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2024

The ceiling in the market is in poor repair, with tiles pushed aside for maintenance, exposing spun insulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front of the store, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large amount of rodent droppings observed beneath market shelves, in basement along walls, and around water heater. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2024
The insulation on overhead ducts in the basement is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2024

The floors in the market, the walk in cooler, and the basement have an accumulation of soil residue and/or rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front of the market, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by numerous droppings on the floor beneath market shelves, and on the floor in the basement, especially beneath wooden pallets. Guardian PCS. Pest control services must be increased to 3 times per week until rodent issue is under control. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Food observed stored on the floor in areas of the market. Food observed stored on the floor beneath hand washing sinks in 2 areas of the market. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor, where it is not subject to splash. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of pallets in the basement have an accumulation of rodent droppings, and dried urine. All pallets contaminated by rodents must be removed from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of wood pallets. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024
The insulation covering ceiling conduits is in poor repair, torn with spun insulation exposed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024

The floor in the basement, and floors beneath shelves in the market have an accumulation of rodent droppings, dried urine, soil residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

The outside areas along the building have an accumulation of trash, litter, leaf litter, vegetation and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed along the outside perimeter of the building. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024
